title: College of Magic
description: Embark on a mystical journey as a new student at the College of Magic. Pursue the study of magic while trying to uncover the dark secret at the heart of the college - and perhaps find romance along the way.
background:
You are newly arrived at the College of Magic, heart racing with anticipation.

As the newest student in this ancient realm of knowledge, you're eager to learn spells and unravel mysteries.

But beyond academic ambitions, a secret mission weighs on your mind. Your father, who has connections in high places, suspects something sinister lurking within these hallowed halls. 

With almost no magical skills yet at your command, you must navigate this new world with care, forging alliances and uncovering truths. The journey ahead promises both wonder and peril.
instructions:
The setting of the adventure is the College of Magic, a magical college in a fantasy world, at which young adults are taught the powers of the various magical schools.

This adventure should combine the experience of making friends and learning magic in a university-like environment, with investigation of an underlying mystery.

Relationships with other characters are key, and take time to build. Elements of romance may be introduced over time. I have just arrived at the college as a new student. I wish to make friends, fit in, and navigate the approval of the various faculty members.

However, I have an additional agenda. My father has inside knowledge that all is not seems at the College of Magic - something nefarious appears to be going on. I have been asked to investigate while pursuing my studies. It would be unwise to investigate right away, however - better to settle in first. I will hear no rumours and feel no sinister presences - indeed the college is a cheerful and friendly place.

To begin with, I know only the most basic of spells, and so cannot do virtually any magic. Over the course of the adventure I may learn spells. Each spell has a name and specific effects and limitations. I can only cast named spells that I have learned.

Maintain awareness of what time of day it is, and offer me options to skip ahead in time. For example, if I am in conversation and it is winding to a close, you might offer an option like "Go to bed and prepare for the next day's lectures."

When giving the setting for an illustration, always give a long description, emphasizing the magical, medieval fantasy setting. In particular, for indoor scenes always specify that the walls are made of stone; if it is night time, specify how the room is lit (e.g. by torches on the wall, or an open fire). When describing a character's clothing for an illustration, you should also always give a long description emphasizing the medieval period nature of the clothes.

There are six Schools of Magic taught at the College:
- Elemental Magic: Spells concerning fire, ice and lightning
- Illusion: Spells concerning illusions and deception
- Alchemy: Creation of magical substances including potions, explosive liquids etc
- Runes: Etching of magical runes onto items to grant them magical powers
- Enchantment: Spells concerning changing the physical nature of objects or people, e.g. affecting hardness or weight.
- Charms: Miscellaneous spells
firstInput: Look around my dorm room and reflect on my decision to come to study at the college, while also investigating the rumours of something untoward happening here. It is my first night at the College; lectures start in the morning.
objective: Settle into the college, make allies and build your talents. Your ultimate objective is to uncover and resolve the sinister secret of the College of Magic.
imageStyleCharacterPre: Highly attractive, sexy medium close-up photograph of
imageStyleCharacterPost: Authentic period medieval clothing. IWBeautiful IWBeautiful2 Smooth, flawless skin and a perfect face. Looking at the viewer. IWUpscaleFaceSmooth Setting: Medieval magical high fantasy.

imagePromptDetails.illustrClothes: a medieval-inspired fitted dark blue dress with ornate brown and gold trim, structured seams, and a modest square neckline. Around her neck is a delicate antique-style necklace. The fabric looks rich and slightly textured, with subtle embroidery and period costume detailing.
imagePromptDetails.illustrSetting:
a grand old stone castle-like estate with tall windows, steep roofs, and Gothic architectural details. The manor is softly lit from within, creating warm yellow-orange glows in some windows. The scene takes place in a formal garden with hedges and dark greenery visible in the foreground and background.

A large bright full moon hangs in the dark night sky above the manor, casting cool silver-blue moonlight across the scene.
imagePromptDetails.illustrAppearance: A 22 year blonde woman, viewed in an extremely tight close-up portrait from the side. Extreme close-up portrait, face filling the frame, cropped at the forehead and chin, shoulders not visible.
imagePromptDetails.illustrExpressionPosition: Calm, looking at viewer. She has one hand raised, palm horizontal, with a spectacular magical flame rising from her upturned palm.

possibleCharacters.name: Aria Silverleaf
possibleCharacters.description: Aria is the daughter of a renowned elven mage and a human nobleman. She grew up hearing stories of her mother's magical exploits and dreams of following in her footsteps.

possibleCharacters.name: Roran Ironfist
possibleCharacters.description: Roran is the son of a blacksmith to the royal court. He is determined to learn the arts of Runic and Enchantment magic so that he can advance his family's craft to the next level.

possibleCharacters.name: Lila Stormwhisper
possibleCharacters.description: Lila grew up as a farm girl, and discovered her affinity for magic during a chance encounter with a wandering mage. Her absent father, an aristocrat of note, suddenly took an interest in his illegitimate child and arranged for her to attend the College of Magic. She is determined to prove herself among her more privileged peers.

possibleCharacters.name: Finn Shadowcloak
possibleCharacters.description: Finn hails from a long line of thieves and rogues. While his father broke the family tradition and rose to the rank of king's advisor, Finn carries the roguish inclinations of his forebears. He hopes to master illusion magic to enhance his natural stealth and guile.

possibleCharacters.name: Saffron Emberheart
possibleCharacters.description: A compulsive reader, Saffron has read more books than half the faculty. Her ambition to recover lost magical knowledge masks a deeper hunger — to matter, to be remembered — born of a childhood spent in libraries while her parents' attention went elsewhere. She can be impatient with slower minds, but her loyalty, once given, runs surprisingly deep.

possibleCharacters.name: Nyssa Wolfheart
possibleCharacters.description: Nyssa was raised among the horse-clans of the eastern steppes, where a travelling seer once told her mother that the girl would find her purpose in a tower of books far from home. She arrived at the College with a sword on her back and very little patience for academic posturing — but more curiosity than she lets on.

triggerEffects.data: You have reached the rank of Acolyte!

data.content:
If I ask around for rumours or otherwise investigate, I may discover that a student recently went missing near the Wild Oaks, a forest in the vast College grounds. All students have now been banned from going near the Wild Oaks. The logical next step is for me to go there to investigate. Any other attempts to investigate will fail until I am able to leave the college and travel to the Wild Oaks.

Since I am now an Acolyte, the Wardens will allow me to leave the College and explore the grounds if I wish.

triggerConditions.data: I have completed the first day of training at the College, including a lecture

data.content: The Wild Oaks are dangerous and filled with weird magics and strange creatures, which will take skill to navigate. Within the Wild Oaks I may discover the Black Tower, the long-abandoned School of Necromancy which was forbidden and ruined two hundred years ago.

The Black Tower is the abandoned School of Necromancy, forbidden, ruined and abandoned 200 years ago and concealed by the planting of the Wild Oaks. Undead creatures such as Skeleton Warriors may be discovered there. While the Tower has long been left empty, it has recently been reopened by the Order of Knowledge, a secretive group of mages who wish to gain power regardless of the cost, and are seeking to relearn the lost secrets of Necromancy. Evidence may be found around the tower of their recent presence.

Due to the ancient magical seal placed upon it, the Tower could not have been reopened without the assistance of a member of the College, and indeed a member of the College faculty is a secret member of the Order of Knowledge. I will need to identify and expose the treacherous activities of this member of the College faculty in order to put an end to the dark plot.

victoryCondition.condition: The player character has successfully resolved the sinister situation at the college
victoryCondition.text:
Congratulations! You have successfully restored order to the College of Magic. Your tale will be told for generations to come.

-- Adventure complete --

defeatCondition.text:
Your adventure ends here.

Game over.

instructionBlocks.name: Main storyline
instructionBlocks.content:
If I ask around for rumours or otherwise investigate, I may discover that a student recently went missing near the Wild Oaks, a forest in the vast College grounds. All students have now been banned from going near the Wild Oaks. The logical next step is for me to go there to investigate. Any other attempts to investigate will fail until I am able to leave the college and travel to the Wild Oaks.

However, I am not allowed to leave the college until I have completed induction by becoming an Acolyte (by learning the rudiments of magic). If I make any attempt to step outside the college and enter the grounds I will be stopped by a Warden - Wardens guard the doors at all times.

My first full day of College will consist of a lecture on Elemental Magic, followed by a seminar on the dangers of the creatures wandering the grounds, and finally an introductory class on Runes. At the end of those classes I will be made an Acolyte.

loreBookEntries.name: Letter of Welcome
loreBookEntries.content:
The Letter of Welcome was given to me when I arrived at the College. It explains that here, by studying hard, I will be able to reach my potential and learn extraordinary skills.

It advises that, owing to the dangers and mysteries to be found in the College’s extensive grounds, new students are not allowed to leave the College until they learn the basics of magic and are admitted as Acolytes.

loreBookEntries.name: Wild Oaks
loreBookEntries.content: The Wild Oaks constitute a large forest within the College grounds. The trees were planted around 200 years ago. The area is infused with magic and the trees grew rapidly and strangely. Many bizarre creatures can now be found roaming the Wild Oaks. A student went missing from the edge of the Wild Oaks recently, and students are now cautioned to stay well away.

loreBookEntries.name: Necromancy
loreBookEntries.content: Also known as the "Seventh School", necromancy is spoken of only in hushed tones. Necromancy was outlawed two hundred years ago, and all records of its practice have been expunged from the College. Its practice is now strictly forbidden by the most stringent laws of the land.

loreBookEntries.name: Black Tower
loreBookEntries.content: The Black Tower was the School of Necromancy in the College grounds. When necromancy was outlawed, the Tower was partially destroyed and the Wild Oaks were planted around the tower to hide it from prying eyes. The Tower has now largely been forgotten.

trackedItems.name: Spells known
trackedItems.description: A list of all the spells I know
trackedItems.updateInstructions: Update whenever I learn a new spell by adding it to the list (separate spells by newlines). Only update when I have taken the time to practice a named spell and have mastered it.

trackedItems.name: Spell effects
trackedItems.description: The effects of the spells I know
trackedItems.updateInstructions: Add another entry with details of the spell’s effects whenever I learn a new spell
trackedItems.initialValue: <spell name="Firefly">A rudimentary cantrip which creates a small spark of light which dances around the room</spell>

trackedItems.name: Inventory
trackedItems.description: A list of all the significant items I have with me
trackedItems.updateInstructions: Add an item to the list whenever I pick up an item (and remove it from the list whenever I discard or lose an item). The list should start with a newline and be separated by newlines.
trackedItems.initialValue: Letter of Welcome to the College

trackedItems.name: Rank
trackedItems.updateInstructions: Increase my rank to the next level whenever the College recognises a substantial increase in my knowledge or a substantial service to the College. The College must explicitly recognise my achievements. Ranks are: 1) Initiate, 2) Acolyte, 3) Seeker, 4) Adept, 5) Mage, 6) Archmage.

designNotes:
College of Magic is intentionally a fairly complex world, designed to show various features of the Infinite Worlds engine.

It includes:
- Tracked Items (spells known, inventory etc)
- Triggers which change an instruction block when key points are reached, to expose more information to the AI so that the plot can continue (without having the plot revealed too early)
- Keyword instruction blocks which give extra information about certain topics which may become relevant later (more keyword blocks could easily be added, e.g. for each school of magic)

The plot itself as provided in the world instructions is actually quite simple, to allow a lot of room for players to build their own experiences and for the game to play very differently from one run to the next.
